title: Why Health Matters
body:
Health matters because it determines what a person can do with their time, talent, and energy. Poor health narrows life quickly.

It also matters because many of the biggest public costs in any society come from preventable illness, chronic disease, and unequal access to care.

A good health system does not just treat crises. It helps people stay well before crises begin.

That makes health one of the most important subjects for both individual life and public policy.
keyPoints: Health is the base layer of capability
Prevention is often more powerful than cure
Chronic disease and ageing make health systems more important
Health literacy improves choices and advocacy
furtherReading: When Breath Becomes Air by Paul Kalanithi
The Spirit Catches You and You Fall Down by Anne Fadiman
